children.heading: .—Establishment
children.label: a .—Establishment
children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall establish an Industrial Base Fund (in this section referred to as the “Fund”).

children.heading: .—Control of Fund
children.label: b .—Control of Fund
children.text: The Fund shall be under the control of the Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition and Sustainment, acting through the Deputy Assistant Secretary of Defense for Manufacturing and Industrial Base Policy.

children.heading: .—Amounts in Fund
children.label: c .—Amounts in Fund
children.text: The Fund shall consist of amounts appropriated or otherwise made available to the Fund.
to support the monitoring and assessment of the industrial base required by chapters 381 through 385 and chapter 389;
to address critical issues in the industrial base relating to urgent operational needs;
to support efforts to expand the industrial base; and
to address supply chain vulnerabilities.

children.heading: .—Use of Fund Subject to Appropriations
children.label: e .—Use of Fund Subject to Appropriations
children.text: The authority of the Secretary of Defense to use the Fund under this section in any fiscal year is subject to the availability of appropriations for that purpose.
Direct obligations from the Fund.
Transfers of monies from the Fund to relevant appropriations of the Department of Defense.

children.label: A
children.text: Castings and forgings.

children.label: B
children.text: Kinetic capabilities, including sensors, targeting systems, and delivery platforms.

children.label: C
children.text: Microelectronics.

children.label: D
children.text: Machine tools, including subtractive, additive, convergent, stamping, forging, abrasives, metrology, and other production equipment.

children.label: E
children.text: Critical minerals, materials, and chemicals.

children.label: F
children.text: The workforce of the defense industrial base.

children.label: G
children.text: Advanced manufacturing (as defined in section 4841(f)) capability and capacity of the defense industrial base, including manufacturing at or near the point of need in the area of responsibility of the United States Indo-Pacific Command.

children.label: H
children.text: Unmanned vehicles, including subsurface, surface, land, air, single use, and attritable unmanned vehicles and associated launch and recovery platforms.

children.label: I
children.text: Manned aircraft.

children.label: J
children.text: Ground systems.

children.label: K
children.text: Power sources.

children.label: L
children.text: Ships or submarines, including technologies and capabilities for the assembly or automation of ships or submarines, new or modernized infrastructure for the construction of new ships or submarines or the maintenance and sustainment or repair of battle damage to ships or submarines.

children.label: M
children.text: Other materiel solutions required to support the operational plans of the United States Indo-Pacific Command.

children.label: N
children.text: Defense space systems.

children.label: O
children.text: Batteries.

children.heading: .—Prohibition on Use in Covered Countries
children.label: 2 .—Prohibition on Use in Covered Countries
children.text: The Secretary may not use the authorities provided by this section for any activity in a covered country.

children.label: i
children.text: the use of the authority for such other purpose is essential to the defense interests of the United States; and

children.label: ii
children.text: without the use of the authority for such other purpose, the defense industrial base cannot reasonably be expected to provide a capability needed by the Department of Defense in a timely manner; and

children.label: B
children.text: not less than 30 days prior to the Secretary using such authorities for such other purpose, submits to the congressional defense committees a report on such determination that includes appropriate explanatory material for such use.

children.heading: .—Grants and Other Incentives for Domestic Industrial Base Capabilities
children.label: h .—Grants and Other Incentives for Domestic Industrial Base Capabilities

children.text: use contracts, grants, or other transaction authorities, including cooperative agreements;
establish incentives for the private sector to develop capabilities in areas of defense interest;

during the five-year period beginning on the date of the enactment of this subsection, make awards to third party entities to support investments in small- and medium-sized entities working in areas of defense interest that would benefit missions of the Department of Defense; and
provide subsidies to offset market manipulation.

children.heading: Defense Industrial Base Purchase Commitment Program.—
children.label: i Defense Industrial Base Purchase Commitment Program.—
A
children.text: for the use or resale of an industrial resource or a critical technology item by the Federal Government;

children.label: B
children.text: to encourage the exploration, development, and mining of strategic and critical materials;

children.label: C
children.text: to support the development of other materials and components;

children.label: D
children.text: for the development of production capabilities; and

children.label: i
children.text: from research and development sponsored by the Federal Government to commercial applications; and

children.label: ii
children.text: from commercial research and development to national defense applications.

children.heading: .—Purchases
children.label: A .—Purchases
children.text: Except as provided by subparagraph (B), purchase commitments under paragraph (1) may be made for such quantities, and on such terms and conditions, including advance payments, and for such periods, but not extending beyond a date that is not more than 10 years from the date on which such purchase was initially made, as the Secretary deems necessary.

children.heading: .—Limitation
children.label: B .—Limitation
children.text: Purchase commitments under paragraph (1) involving higher than established ceiling prices (or if no such established ceiling prices exist, currently prevailing market prices) or that result in an anticipated loss on resale shall not be made, unless it is determined that supply of the materials to be purchased under such purchase commitments could not be effectively increased or provisioned at lower prices or on terms more favorable to the Federal Government, or that such purchases are necessary to assure the availability to the United States of overseas supplies.

children.label: i
children.text: under generally fair and equitable ceiling prices, for any raw or nonprocessed material or component, there will result a decrease in supplies from high-cost sources of such material and that the continuation of such supplies from such sources is necessary to carry out the objectives of this section; or

children.label: ii
children.text: an increase in cost of transportation of such material is temporary and threatens to impair maximum production or supply in any area at stable prices of such material.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a finding described in clause (i) of such subparagraph, supplies from high-cost sources of such material do not decrease; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a finding described in clause (ii) of such subparagraph with respect to one or more areas, that maximum production or supply of such material at stable prices in each such area is maintained, as applicable.

children.heading: Installation of equipment in industrial facilities.—
children.label: 4 Installation of equipment in industrial facilities.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to take an action described in subparagraph (B) if the Secretary determines that such action will aid the defense interests of the United States.

children.label: i
children.text: procuring and installing additional equipment, facilities, processes or improvements to plants, factories, and other industrial facilities owned by the Federal Government;

children.label: ii
children.text: procuring and installing equipment, including equipment owned by the Federal Government, in privately owned plants, factories, and other industrial facilities;

children.label: iii
children.text: providing for the modification, expansion, or construction of new privately owned facilities, including modifications or improvements to production processes, when taking actions under this subsection or subsection (h);

children.label: iv
children.text: selling or otherwise transferring equipment owned by the Federal Government and installed under this subsection to the owners of such plants, factories, or other industrial facilities;

children.label: v
children.text: constructing facilities for the purposes described in section subsection (g)(1); and

children.label: vi
children.text: applying contracts, grants, or other transactions authorities.

children.heading: Excess metals, minerals, materials, and components.—
children.label: 5 Excess metals, minerals, materials, and components.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Metals, minerals, materials, and components acquired pursuant to this subsection which are excess to the needs of programs under this section, as determined by the Secretary, shall be transferred to the National Defense Stockpile established by the Strategic and Critical Materials Stock Piling Act ( et seq.), or other national reserves if available, if the Secretary deems such transfer to be in the public interest.50 U.S.C. 98

children.heading: .—Transfers at no charge
children.label: B .—Transfers at no charge
children.text: Transfers made pursuant to this paragraph shall be made without charge against or reimbursement from funds appropriated for the purposes of the Strategic and Critical Materials Stock Piling Act ( et seq.), or other national reserves if available, except that costs incident to such transfer, other than acquisition costs, shall be paid or reimbursed from such funds.50 U.S.C. 98

children.label: i
children.text: such metal, mineral, material, or component is deemed to have been determined to be strategic and critical under section 3(a) of the Strategic and Critical Materials Stock Piling Act (); and50 U.S.C. 98b(a)

children.label: ii
children.text: the Stockpile Manager of the National Defense Stockpile is deemed to have determines there is a shortfall of such materials in the National Defense Stockpile.

children.heading: .—Substitutes
children.label: 6 .—Substitutes
children.text: The Secretary may make provision for the development and qualification of substitutes for strategic and critical materials, components, critical technology items, and other industrial resources if and to the extent the Secretary determines that such development and qualification is in the interest of national security.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary may provide appropriate incentives to develop, maintain, modernize, restore, and expand the productive capacities of sources for strategic and critical materials, components, critical technology items, and industrial resources essential for the execution of the national security strategy of the United States.

children.heading: Strategic and critical materials, components, and critical technology items.—
children.label: 2 Strategic and critical materials, components, and critical technology items.—

children.heading: .—Maintenance of reliable sources of supply
children.label: A .—Maintenance of reliable sources of supply
children.text: The Secretary shall take appropriate actions to ensure that strategic and critical materials, components, critical technology items, and industrial resources are available from reliable sources when and as needed to meet the requirements of the Department of Defense during peacetime, mobilization, and national emergency (as defined in section 12 of the Strategic and Critical Materials Act (50 USC 98h-3)).

children.label: i
children.text: restricting contract solicitations to reliable sources;

children.label: ii
children.text: stockpiling or placing into reserve strategic and critical materials, components, and critical technology items;

children.label: iii
children.text: planning for necessary long lead times for acquiring such materials, components, and items; and

children.label: iv
children.text: developing and qualifying substitutes for such materials, components, and items.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Not later than , and annually thereafter, the Secretary shall submit to the congressional defense committee a report evaluating investments made and any other activities carried out using amounts in the Fund during the previous fiscal year.October 15, 2026

children.label: A
children.text: measures of the effectiveness of the investments and activities described in such paragraph in meeting the needs of the Department of Defense and the defense industrial base;

children.label: B
children.text: an evaluation of the return on investment of all ongoing investments from the Fund; and

children.label: C
children.text: a description of efforts to coordinate activities carried out using amounts in the Fund with activities to support the defense industrial base carried out under other authorities.

children.heading: .—Advice
children.label: 3 .—Advice
children.text: In preparing a report required by paragraph (1), the Secretary shall take into account the advice of the defense industry and such other individuals as the Secretary considers relevant.

children.heading: Coordination With Other Defense Industrial Base Activities.—
children.label: l Coordination With Other Defense Industrial Base Activities.—

children.text: activities carried out using amounts in the Defense Production Act Fund under section 304 of the Defense Production Act of 1950 (50 U.S.C.4534);
activities of the Office of Strategic Capital; and
any other efforts designed to enhance the defense industrial base.

children.label: A
children.text: the Russian Federation;

children.label: B
children.text: the Republic of Cuba;

children.label: C
children.text: the Bolivarian Republic of Venezuela;

children.label: D
children.text: the Democratic People’s Republic of Korea;

children.label: E
children.text: the Islamic Republic of Iran; and

children.label: F
children.text: the People’s Republic of China.

children.label: A
children.text: the United States or any territory or possession of the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: a country of the national technology and industrial base, as defined in section 4801; or

children.label: C
children.text: a qualifying country, as defined in section 225.003 of the Department of Defense Supplement to the Federal Acquisition Regulation or any successor regulation.
The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Defense.
The term “strategic and critical materials” has the meaning given that term in section 12(1) of the Strategic and Critical Materials Stock Piling Act ().50 U.S.C. 98h–3(1)
